summary: fetchcraft pulls install-time tarball from plain-HTTP dynamic-DNS host
details: package.json declares `node-runtime-utils` as a direct dependency whose version specifier is a plain-HTTP tarball URL on a DuckDNS dynamic-DNS host: `"node-runtime-utils": "http://noderuntime.duckdns.org:3258/pkg/node-runtime-utils-1.0.0.tgz"`. On `npm install fetchcraft`, npm fetches this tarball over cleartext HTTP from an attacker-controlled, dynamic-DNS, nonstandard-port endpoint and installs it — executing any preinstall/install/postinstall lifecycle scripts it ships, with no integrity check, no version pin, no registry vetting, and a backing IP the operator can rotate at will. The rest of the package implements a plausible 'production-grade fetch utilities' library (FetchClient, retry, LRU cache, pagination) that serves as a benign-looking cover for the hostile dependency edge.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
